Title: Uptake mechanism of iron-phytosiderophore from the soil based on the structure of yellow stripe transporter

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: root surface/plasma membrane-mediated uptake from soil into root cells
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: iron acquisition under alkaline/calcareous soil conditions, supporting plant growth and preventing iron deficiency chlorosis
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: YS1/YSL family metal-phytosiderophore transporter; membrane transporter
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: cryo-EM structures of apo and substrate-bound states; insect cell-based 55Fe-DMA uptake assay; site-directed mutagenesis; disulfide crosslinking; MD simulation
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: predominantly functions at root surface for Fe(III)-MA uptake
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: predominantly functions at root surface for Fe(III)-MA uptake
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: mediates uptake of Fe(III)-DMA; mutations in substrate-binding or proton-coupling residues impair transport to near-background levels
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HvYS1 forms a homodimeric YS1/YSL transporter with an outward-open central cavity. 2. HvYS1 directly recognizes and transports Fe(III)-DMA via a specific extracellular-facing binding pocket. 3. Binding-site residues F130, Y451, K482, and Y551 are required for Fe(III)-DMA uptake. 4. Asp446, Asp490, and Asp494 are implicated in proton-coupled transport, with mutations impairing activity. 5. HvYS1 also binds Fe(III)-PDMA in essentially the same pocket, supporting use of PDMA as a functional fertilizer analog.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: alkaline/calcareous soil; Fe(III)-DMA or Fe(III)-PDMA substrate supply
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: provides a structural framework to design improved synthetic phytosiderophore fertilizers such as PDMA and potentially engineer crop iron uptake systems for calcareous soils
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Classified as Common Gene because HvYS1 is a transporter rather than a catalytic pathway enzyme or transcriptional regulator. HvYS1 predominantly transports Fe(III)-mugineic acids with strict specificity. Structural study suggests proton-coupled elevator-like transport. Dimer stabilization is enhanced by cholesterol hemisuccinate/phytosterol-like molecules, although monomeric function could not be fully excluded.
